Sushi Kim 2
HomeMenuAboutReservationsContact

Our Story

Pittsburgh's Korean and Sushi restaurant since 1990.

Sushi Kim opened in the Strip District in 1990. After more than two decades there, we moved to 110 Smithfield St. downtown after COVID and reopened as Sushi Kim 2.

The menu got shorter, but the favorites stayed.

Korean comfort food on one side, fresh-cut sushi on the other. That's always been the formula.

How we cook

Three things we don't compromise.

01

The food comes first.

Every stew starts in the morning. Fish is cut by hand daily. We don't rush it.

02

Fresh, every day.

Fish arrives daily. Kimchi is fermented in-house. Banchan changes with the season.

03

Consistent.

The recipes haven't changed much in thirty years. That's the point.

Timeline

30+ years, in brief.

1990

Doors open in the Strip District

Sushi Kim opens in Pittsburgh's Strip District with a Korean menu and a small sushi bar.

2008

The galbi recipe is set

After years of iteration, the galbi marinade is finalized. It has not been changed since.

2013

Second-generation chefs join the kitchen

The Kim children join the line, trained by their parents since childhood.

2021

Sushi Kim 2 opens downtown

After COVID, we move to 110 Smithfield St. and reopen as Sushi Kim 2. The menu got shorter, but the favorites stayed.

2023

4.8 stars, 200+ reviews

Pittsburgh keeps coming back. We try to earn it every day.

2026

30+ years and counting

Still family-run. Still cooking slow. Still at 110 Smithfield.

The soups

Jjigae is at the center of the menu. Korean stew cooked low and slow — rich broth, bold flavor. It's the kind of food Pittsburgh winters were made for.

Come eat with us

Korean comfort food and fresh-cut sushi in the heart of downtown Pittsburgh.

Sushi Kim 2
